'''Max Jowitt''' (born 6 May 1997) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]]. 

He has spent time on loan from [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield]] at the [[Dewsbury Rams]] the [[2017 RFL Championship|Championship]], and [[Oxford Rugby League|Oxford]] and the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]].

He holds the record in British rugby league for most points scored in a single season with 500 in 2024.{{cn|date=March 2026}}

==Background==
Jowitt was born in [[Wakefield]], [[West Yorkshire, England]]. He is the son of Ian Jowitt who also played for Wakefield Trinity in the 1980s and 1990s.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/ian-jowitt/summary.html|title = Ian Jowitt - Career Stats & Summary - Rugby League Project}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/max-jowitt/summary.html|title = Max Jowitt - Career Stats & Summary - Rugby League Project}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/44524643|title = Jowitt signs three-year Wakefield deal|work = BBC Sport}}</ref>

==Career==
Jowitt made his Wakefield Trinity Wildcats début on 15 August 2014 in a Super League match against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Langtree Park]] playing at {{rlp|fb}}. He was awarded the [[Albert Goldthorpe Medal|Albert Goldthorpe]] Rookie of the Year Medal in 2016, but lost his first team place in 2017 following the arrival of [[Scott Grix]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Jowitt determined to prove himself at Wakefield however necessary |url=https://www.totalrl.com/jowitt-determined-prove-wakefield-however-necessary/ |website=Total Rugby League |access-date=3 October 2023 |date=3 January 2018}}</ref>

Jowitt played 15 games for Wakefield Trinity in the [[2023 Super League season]] season as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated to the [[RFL Championship]] which ended their 24-year stay in the top flight.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/20876/12962152/super-league-wakefield-trinity-relegated-after-loss-at-leigh-leopards-st-helens-and-wigan-warriors-win/|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity relegated after loss at Leigh Leopards, St Helens and Wigan Warriors win|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

The following season, Jowitt became the first British player to score 500 points in a season (26 tries and 198 goals) in 34 matches as Wakefield won the [[2024 RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[2024 RFL 1895 Cup|1895 Cup]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Championship Grand Final: Wakefield Trinity 36-0 Toulouse |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cqjr5lvxk4lo |work=BBC Sport |date=19 October 2024}}</ref>
On 25 May 2025, Jowitt scored a try and kicked ten goals in Wakefield Trinity's 72-10 victory over Salford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/czelylrnx5xo|title=Wakefield thrash Salford to equal record league win|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Jowitt played 21 games for Wakefield Trinity in the 2025 Super League season which saw the club finish sixth on the table and qualify for the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/rugby-league-news-what-next-for-wakefield-trinity-after-incredible-super-league-return-ends-in-play-off-loss-108937/|title=What next for Wakefield Trinity after incredible Super League return ends in play-off loss|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com}}</ref>

==International career==
In July 2018 he was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref>
